DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-10-2017 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Defendant Daniel Schwartz's demurrer to both causes of action in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff DTA Master, LLC is overruled as to both causes of action. Neither cause of action is barred by the California Uniform Trade Secrets Act because both allege wrongdoing separate from and in addition to misuse of any information. Liberally construed, the first amended complaint adequately alleges that: 1) Mr. Schwartz owed fiduciary duties to DTA by virtue of Mr. Schwartz' business arrangement with Mr. Fishman while Mr. Fishman was a co-owner and principal of DTA and 2) Mr. Schwartz knowingly usurped business opportunities belonging to DTA. The first cause of action adequately alleges a claim for breach of fiduciary duty based on the foregoing allegations. The second cause of action adequately alleges a violation of the UCL per the unlawful prong based on the allegation that Mr. Schwartz breached his fiduciary duties to DTA. Relying on Silvaco Data Systems v. Intel Corp. (2010) 184 Cal. App. 4th 210, counsel for Mr. Schwartz argued at the May 3, 2010 hearing that the demurrer should be sustained to the portion of the second cause of action for violation of the UCL with respect to the time period after DTA alleged that Mr. Schwartz had a fiduciary relationship with DTA. The court took the demurrer under submission to review Silvaco on this point and, having done so, concludes that nothing in Silvaco departs from the rule that a demurrer only lies to the entirety of a cause of action and thus the court rejects Mr. Schwartz' request that his demurrer be sustained to a portion of the second cause of action. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen CSR #8189 =(302/HK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Statutory Attorneys Fees
Off Calendar Apr-25-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. STEPHANIE A. SPERBER OF THE CALLAHAN & BLAINE LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 1-714-241-4444 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. OLGA SAVAGE OF THE HARTNETT SMITH & PAETKALI, TEL. NO. 1-650-260-1258 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant Christopher Walsh's motion for statutory attorneys' fees is denied. While there is no persuasive evidence on the record on this motion, that Mr. Walsh misappropriated any trade secrets (or confidential information) of plaintiff DTA Master, LLC, the evidence on this motion does not support the conclusion that DTA filed its misappropriation of trade secrets claim against Mr. Walsh in subjective bad faith. Rather, the most reasonable interpretation of the evidence is that, albeit apparently misguided, DTA sued Mr. Walsh based on its belief that there was a substantial likelihood that Mr. Walsh had misused DTA's claimed trade secrets rather than for the improper purpose to obtain leverage against Mr. Fishman and/or to punish Mr. Walsh for allying himself or otherwise favoring Mr. Fishman. DTA's request for reconsideration of the order determining Mr. Walsh the prevailing party for purposes of the costs statutes is denied. There is no basis to disturb that order. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen CSR #8189 =302/hk

MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF DTA MASTER, LLC'S MOTION TO STRIKE OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, TAX DEFENDANT CHRISTOPHER WALSHS MEMORANDUM OF COSTS

Appearances: Stephanie A. Sperber of Callahan & Blaine, APLC (714.241.4444) appearing via Court Call for plaintiff DTA Master LLC; Olga Savage of Hartnett Smith & Paetkau (650.260.1258) for defendant Christopher Walsh; Charles T. Graves of Wilson Sonsini Goodrich & Rosati (415.947.2109) for defendant Daniel Schwartz

Argued and the Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Denied in its entirety. Adler v. Vaicius (1993) 21 Cal. App. 4th 1770 is on-point authority holding that where a plaintiff obtains TRO relief and then dismisses the case without obtaining any further relief, the dismissed defendant, not the plaintiff, is the prevailing party per CCP 1032(a)(4). Even if the TRO is treated as "other than monetary relief" and the second sentence of CCP 1032(a)(4) is applicable, the court exercises its discretion to determine that Mr. Walsh is the prevailing party because the benefits he obtained by not having to face possibility liability on the multiplicity of claims alleged against him far outweighs the benefits obtained by DTA by the TRO. The demurrer and "urgent service" costs were reasonably necessary and the costs of the computer expert appointed by the court are recoverable in the court's discretion per CCP 1033.5(c)(4) and not prohibited by CCP 1033.5(b)(1). Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Kelly DeYoung, CSR 6780 (kdeyoung@sftc.org) (302/HEK)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-02-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Stephanie A. Sperber, of Callahan & Blaine, Tel. 714.241.4444, for plaintiff DTA Master, LLC;
Katherine L. Henderson, of Wilson Sonsini Goodrich & Rosati, Tel. 415.947.2000, for defendant Daniel Schwartz;
Charles Graves, of of Wilson Sonsini Goodrich & Rosati, Tel. 415.947.2109, for defendant Daniel Schwartz.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT DANIEL SCHWARTZ DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT, to all causes of action alleged against him in the complaint filed by plaintiff DTA Master, LLC is sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to the second cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty, third cause of action for misappropriation of trade secrets and fourth cause of action for violation of the Unfair Competition Law and sustained without leave to amend as to the fifth cause of action for conspiracy. DTA Master has not sufficiently alleged the existence of a joint venture or partnership between Mr. Schwartz and DTA to support a fiduciary duty owed by Mr. Schwartz to DTA and a claim for violation of the UCL. Nor has DTA sufficiently alleged that Mr. Schwartz acquired or used the asserted trade secret information with the requisite wrongful intent as required by CUTSA. Conspiracy is not a cause of action. DTA is given leave to cure these deficiencies if it can do so in good faith. If it does cure these deficiencies, DTA's claims for breach of fiduciary duty and violation of the UCL will withstand a further demurrer based on the argument that those claims are preempted by CUTSA if the first amended complaint includes the allegations that Mr. Schwartz knowingly usurped the corporate opportunities of DTA. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.
